summary.intent: Remove the legacy Session table and all hook-handler infrastructure from the gossamer project, leaving only the indexer and poll-loop-based server.
summary.outcome: Deleted handler.ts and index.ts entirely, rewrote db.ts and serve.ts as thin entry points, purged Session/Event/AgentParent/PendingToolUse and all v1 checkpoint models from the Prisma schema, removed corresponding server endpoints and search.ts joins, and stripped Anthropic SDK dependencies from package.json. Build passes clean.

repo: The project has two distinct data layers: a legacy hook-handler layer (Session, Event, AgentParent, PendingToolUse, Checkpoint* tables) and the current indexer layer (CheckpointSessionMetadata, ShadowSession). Only the latter is still active.

The hook handler stored per-session ephemeral state (cwd, repoRoot, gitUser, summary, keywords) in the Session table as a cross-invocation scratchpad — all of it was redundant with data already in the Event table or recomputable on demand.
summary and keywords were written as columns on the UserPromptSubmit Event row (writeEventLog lines 824–829), making the Session table's copies fully redundant.
search.ts used a LEFT JOIN on Session solely for gitUserName/gitUserEmail enrichment — those fields are now dropped from search results.
package.json had @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk, @anthropic-ai/sdk, and commander as dependencies only needed by the deleted handler/index entrypoints.

code.finding: applyEvent deletes state.sessions[session_id] on SessionEnd, meaning ended sessions were already removed from in-memory state but writeStateToDb never issued a corresponding DELETE, causing stale rows to accumulate indefinitely.
db.session.findUnique was called to look up cwd of a recently-ended session for auto-linking — replaced by JSON.parse(recentEnd.data).cwd since the full HookInput is stored in the Event table.
writeEventLog writes summary/keywords as columns on the UserPromptSubmit Event row, making the Session table copy redundant.
LEFT JOIN Session was the only place gitUserName/gitUserEmail appeared in query results; removed along with those fields from the result type.

workflow: The session confirmed scope by auditing actual DB model usage with targeted grep patterns (db.(checkpoint|session|event|...)\b) before deciding what to delete.
Build verification (npm run build) after each major edit caught type errors early, preventing late-stage integration failures.

The user's clarification ('We don't care about the hook handler at all anymore') pivoted the task from a targeted cleanup to a wholesale removal — auditing the indexer and server first before cutting was the right sequencing.

friction: server.ts required multiple read/edit passes to locate exact line boundaries for the old v1 endpoint block before it could be deleted cleanly.

The task initially started as a narrow stale-row cleanup but expanded significantly after the user revealed the hook handler was entirely out of scope — earlier scoping would have avoided the initial partial fix.
open_items: prisma db push has not been run yet — the old tables (Session, Event, AgentParent, PendingToolUse, Checkpoint*) still exist in the live database and will be dropped on next push.
gitUserName/gitUserEmail were dropped from search results with no replacement — if that enrichment is needed it would have to come from UserPromptSubmit Event data.

parentSessionId was noted as redundant (already in AgentParent table) but that observation predated the decision to drop everything; worth confirming the AgentParent table was indeed removed and nothing depends on that relationship.
